v.CNV: 有一个人名叫亚拿尼亚，同他妻子撒非拉，把田产卖了。
v.ESV: But a man named Ananias, with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property,
v.GAE: 아나니아라 하는 사람이 그의 아내 삽비라와 더불어 소유를 팔아
v.KJV: But a certain man named Ananias, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession,
v.NASB: But a man named Ananias, with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property,
v.NIV: Now a man named Ananias, together with his wife Sapphira, also sold a piece of property.
v.NRSV: But a man named Ananias, with the consent of his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property;
v.SAENEW: 그런데 아나니아라는 사람이 그의 아내 삽비라와 함께 소유를 팔아서,
v.WLB: 아나니아라는 사람은 그의 아내 삽비라와 함께 재산을 팔았습니다.

v.CNV: 他私底下把钱留了一部分，妻子也知道这件事。他把其余的一部分带来，放在使徒的脚前。
v.ESV: and with his wife's knowledge he kept back for himself some of the proceeds and brought only a part of it and laid it at the apostles' feet.
v.GAE: 그 값에서 얼마를 감추매 그 아내도 알더라 얼마만 가져다가 사도들의 발 앞에 두니
v.KJV: And kept back part of the price, his wife also being privy to it, and brought a certain part, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
v.NASB: and kept back some of the price for himself, with his wife's full knowledge, and bringing a portion of it, he laid it at the apostles' feet.
v.NIV: With his wife’s full knowledge he kept back part of the money for himself, but brought the rest and put it at the apostles’ feet.
v.NRSV: with his wife’s knowledge, he kept back some of the proceeds, and brought only a part and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
v.SAENEW: 그 값의 얼마를 따로 떼어놓았는데, 그의 아내도 이것을 알고 있었다. 그는 떼어놓고 난 나머지를 가져다가, 사도들의 발 앞에 놓았다.
v.WLB: 그는 그 돈의 일부를 떼어 자기 것으로 숨겨 두고는 나머지를 사도들의 발 앞에 가져와 바쳤습니다. 그의 아내도 이 사실을 다 알고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 彼得说：“亚拿尼亚，为什么撒但充满了你的心，使你欺骗圣灵，私底下把卖地的钱留了一部分呢？
v.ESV: But Peter said, “Ananias, why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit and to keep back for yourself part of the proceeds of the land?
v.GAE: 베드로가 이르되 아나니아야 어찌하여 사탄이 네 마음에 가득하여 네가 성령을 속이고 땅 값 얼마를 감추었느냐
v.KJV: But Peter said, Ananias, why hath Satan filled thine heart to lie to the Holy Ghost, and to keep back part of the price of the land?
v.NASB: But Peter said, 'Ananias, why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit and to keep back some of the price of the land?
v.NIV: Then Peter said, “Ananias, how is it that Satan has so filled your heart that you have lied to the Holy Spirit and have kept for yourself some of the money you received for the land?
v.NRSV: “Ananias,” Peter asked, “why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit and to keep back part of the proceeds of the land?
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 베드로가 이렇게 말하였다. "아나니아는 들으시오. 어찌하여 그대의 마음이 사탄에게 홀려서, 그대가 성령을 속이고 땅 값의 얼마를 몰래 떼어놓았소?
v.WLB: 그러자 베드로가 말했습니다. “아나니아야, 어떻게 네가 사탄에게 마음을 빼앗겨 성령을 속이고 땅 값으로 받은 돈의 일부를 네 것으로 몰래 숨겨 놓았느냐?

v.CNV: 田地还没有卖，不是你自己的吗？既然卖了，所得的钱不是由你作主吗？你为什么存心这样作呢？你这不是欺骗人，而是欺骗　神。”
v.ESV: While it remained unsold, did it not remain your own? And after it was sold, was it not at your disposal? Why is it that you have contrived this deed in your heart? You have not lied to man but to God.”
v.GAE: 땅이 그대로 있을 때에는 네 땅이 아니며 판 후에도 네 마음대로 할 수가 없더냐 어찌하여 이 일을 네 마음에 두었느냐 사람에게 거짓말한 것이 아니요 하나님께로다
v.KJV: Whiles it remained, was it not thine own? and after it was sold, was it not in thine own power? why hast thou conceived this thing in thine heart? thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.
v.NASB: 'While it remained unsold, did it not remain your own? And after it was sold, was it not under your control? Why is it that you have conceived this deed in your heart? You have not lied to men but to God.'
v.NIV: Didn’t it belong to you before it was sold? And after it was sold, wasn’t the money at your disposal? What made you think of doing such a thing? You have not lied just to human beings but to God.”
v.NRSV: While it remained unsold, did it not remain your own? And after it was sold, were not the proceeds at your disposal? How is it that you have contrived this deed in your heart? You did not lie to us but to God!”
v.SAENEW: 그 땅은 팔리기 전에도 그대의 것이 아니었소? 또 팔린 뒤에도 그대 마음대로 할 수 있었던 것이 아니었소? 그런데 어찌하여 이런 일을 할 마음을 먹었소? 그대는 사람을 속인 것이 아니라 하나님을 속인 것이오."
v.WLB: 그 땅은 팔기 전에도 네 소유였고 또 팔고 난 뒤에도 네 마음대로 처분할 수 있지 않았느냐? 그런데 왜 이런 일을 마음에 품었느냐? 너는 사람을 속인 것이 아니라 하나님을 속인 것이다.”

v.CNV: 亚拿尼亚一听见这话，就仆倒断了气。所有听见的人都十分害怕。
v.ESV: When Ananias heard these words, he fell down and breathed his last. And great fear came upon all who heard of it.
v.GAE: 아나니아가 이 말을 듣고 엎드러져 혼이 떠나니 이 일을 듣는 사람이 다 크게 두려워하더라
v.KJV: And Ananias hearing these words fell down, and gave up the ghost: and great fear came on all them that heard these things.
v.NASB: And as he heard these words, Ananias fell down and breathed his last; and great fear came over all who heard of it.
v.NIV: When Ananias heard this, he fell down and died. And great fear seized all who heard what had happened.
v.NRSV: Now when Ananias heard these words, he fell down and died. And great fear seized all who heard of it.
v.SAENEW: 아나니아는 이 말을 듣고, 그 자리에서 쓰러져서 숨졌다. 이 소문을 듣는 사람은 모두 크게 두려워하였다.
v.WLB: 아나니아는 이 말을 듣자마자 쓰러져 죽었습니다. 그리고 이 일을 듣는 사람들은 모두 두려움에 사로잡혔습니다.

v.CNV: 有几个青年人来把他包好，抬出去埋了。
v.ESV: The young men rose and wrapped him up and carried him out and buried him.
v.GAE: 젊은 사람들이 일어나 시신을 싸서 메고 나가 장사하니라
v.KJV: And the young men arose, wound him up, and carried him out, and buried him.
v.NASB: The young men got up and covered him up, and after carrying him out, they buried him.
v.NIV: Then some young men came forward, wrapped up his body, and carried him out and buried him.
v.NRSV: The young men came and wrapped up his body, then carried him out and buried him.
v.SAENEW: 젊은이들이 일어나, 그 시체를 싸서 메고 나가서, 장사를 지냈다.
v.WLB: 그때 청년들이 들어와 그 시체를 싸서 들고 나가 묻어 주었습니다.

v.CNV: 大约三小时之后，亚拿尼亚的妻子进来，还不知道发生了什么事。
v.ESV: After an interval of about three hours his wife came in, not knowing what had happened.
v.GAE: 세 시간쯤 지나 그의 아내가 그 일어난 일을 알지 못하고 들어오니
v.KJV: And it was about the space of three hours after, when his wife, not knowing what was done, came in.
v.NASB: Now there elapsed an interval of about three hours, and his wife came in, not knowing what had happened.
v.NIV: About three hours later his wife came in, not knowing what had happened.
v.NRSV: After an interval of about three hours his wife came in, not knowing what had happened.
v.SAENEW: 세 시간쯤 지나서, 아나니아의 아내가 그 동안에 일어난 일을 알지 못하고 들어왔다.
v.WLB: 세 시간쯤 지나서 그의 아내가 무슨 일이 일어났는지도 모르고 들어왔습니다.

v.CNV: 彼得问她：“你告诉我，你们卖田地的钱，就是这么多吗？”她说：“是的，就是这么多。”
v.ESV: And Peter said to her, “Tell me whether you sold the land for so much.” And she said, “Yes, for so much.”
v.GAE: 베드로가 이르되 그 땅 판 값이 이것뿐이냐 내게 말하라 하니 이르되 예 이것뿐이라 하더라
v.KJV: And Peter answered unto her, Tell me whether ye sold the land for so much? And she said, Yea, for so much.
v.NASB: And Peter responded to her, 'Tell me whether you sold the land for such and such a price?' And she said, 'Yes, that was the price.'
v.NIV: Peter asked her, “Tell me, is this the price you and Ananias got for the land?” “Yes,” she said, “that is the price.”
v.NRSV: Peter said to her, “Tell me whether you and your husband sold the land for such and such a price.” And she said, “Yes, that was the price.”
v.SAENEW: 베드로가 그 여자에게 물었다. "그대들이 판 땅값이 이것뿐이오? 어디 말해 보시오." 그 여자가 대답하였다. "예, 그것뿐입니다."
v.WLB: 베드로가 그녀에게 물었습니다. “말해 보아라. 너와 아나니아가 땅을 팔아 받은 돈이 이것뿐인가?” 삽비라가 대답했습니다. “네, 그게 전부입니다.”

v.CNV: 彼得说：“你们为什么串同试探主的灵呢？你看，埋你丈夫的人的脚，已经到了门口，他们也要把你抬出去。”
v.ESV: But Peter said to her, “How is it that you have agreed together to test the Spirit of the Lord? Behold, the feet of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out.”
v.GAE: 베드로가 이르되 너희가 어찌 함께 꾀하여 주의 영을 시험하려 하느냐 보라 네 남편을 장사하고 오는 사람들의 발이 문 앞에 이르렀으니 또 너를 메어 내가리라 하니
v.KJV: Then Peter said unto her, How is it that ye have agreed together to tempt the Spirit of the Lord? behold, the feet of them which have buried thy husband are at the door, and shall carry thee out.
v.NASB: Then Peter said to her, 'Why is it that you have agreed together to put the Spirit of the Lord to the test? Behold, the feet of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out as well.'
v.NIV: Peter said to her, “How could you conspire to test the Spirit of the Lord? Listen! The feet of the men who buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out also.”
v.NRSV: Then Peter said to her, “How is it that you have agreed together to put the Spirit of the Lord to the test? Look, the feet of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out.”
v.SAENEW: 베드로가 그 여자에게 말하였다. "왜 그대들 내외는 서로 공모해서 주님의 영을 시험하려고 하였소? 보시오. 그대의 남편을 묻은 사람들의 발이 막 문에 다다랐으니, 그들이 또 그대를 메고 나갈 것이오."
v.WLB: 베드로가 그녀에게 말했습니다. “너희가 어째서 서로 짜고 주의 영을 시험하려고 하느냐? 보아라. 네 남편을 묻은 사람들이 문 앞에 있으니 이번에는 너를 메고 나갈 것이다.”

v.CNV: 她立刻就仆倒在彼得脚前，断了气。那些青年人进来，发现她死了，把她也抬出去，埋在她丈夫旁边。
v.ESV: Immediately she fell down at his feet and breathed her last. When the young men came in they found her dead, and they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
v.GAE: 곧 그가 베드로의 발 앞에 엎드러져 혼이 떠나는지라 젊은 사람들이 들어와 죽은 것을 보고 메어다가 그의 남편 곁에 장사하니
v.KJV: Then fell she down straightway at his feet, and yielded up the ghost: and the young men came in, and found her dead, and, carrying her forth, buried her by her husband.
v.NASB: And immediately she fell at his feet and breathed her last, and the young men came in and found her dead, and they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
v.NIV: At that moment she fell down at his feet and died. Then the young men came in and, finding her dead, carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
v.NRSV: Immediately she fell down at his feet and died. When the young men came in they found her dead, so they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 그 여자는 그 자리에서 베드로의 발 앞에 쓰러져서 숨졌다. 젊은이들이 들어와서, 그 여자가 죽은 것을 보고서, 메어다가 그 남편 곁에 묻었다.
v.WLB: 바로 그 순간 삽비라도 그 발 앞에 쓰러져 죽었습니다. 그러자 청년들이 들어와 그가 죽은 것을 보고는 메고 나가 그 남편 곁에 묻었습니다.

v.CNV: 全体会众和所有听见这事的人，都很害怕。
v.ESV: And great fear came upon the whole church and upon all who heard of these things.
v.GAE: 온 교회와 이 일을 듣는 사람들이 다 크게 두려워하니라
v.KJV: And great fear came upon all the church, and upon as many as heard these things.
v.NASB: And great fear came over the whole church, and over all who heard of these things.
v.NIV: Great fear seized the whole church and all who heard about these events.
v.NRSV: And great fear seized the whole church and all who heard of these things.
v.SAENEW: 온 교회와 이 사건을 듣는 사람들은, 모두 크게 두려워하였다.
v.WLB: 온 교회와 이 일에 대해 소문을 들은 모든 사람들은 큰 두려움에 사로잡혔습니다.

v.CNV: 主借着使徒的手，在民间行了许多神迹奇事。他们都同心聚集在所罗门廊下，
v.ESV: Now many signs and wonders were regularly done among the people by the hands of the apostles. And they were all together in Solomon's Portico.
v.GAE: 사도들의 손을 통하여 민간에 표적과 기사가 많이 일어나매 믿는 사람이 다 마음을 같이하여 솔로몬 행각에 모이고
v.KJV: And by the hands of the apostles were many signs and wonders wrought among the people; (and they were all with one accord in Solomon’s porch.
v.NASB: At the hands of the apostles many signs and wonders were taking place among the people; and they were all with one accord in Solomon's portico.
v.NIV: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. And all the believers used to meet together in Solomon’s Colonnade.
v.NRSV: Now many signs and wonders were done among the people through the apostles. And they were all together in Solomon’s Portico.
v.SAENEW: 사도들의 손을 거쳐서 많은 표징과 놀라운 일이 백성 가운데서 일어났다. 그들은 모두 한 마음이 되어서, 솔로몬 행각에 모이곤 하였다.
v.WLB: 사도들은 사람들 가운데 많은 기적들과 표적들을 일으켰습니다. 그리고 믿는 사람들은 모두 한마음으로 솔로몬 행각에 모이곤 했습니다.

v.CNV: 其余的人，没有一个敢接近他们，可是民众都很敬重他们。
v.ESV: None of the rest dared join them, but the people held them in high esteem.
v.GAE: 그 나머지는 감히 그들과 상종하는 사람이 없으나 백성이 칭송하더라
v.KJV: And of the rest durst no man join himself to them: but the people magnified them.
v.NASB: But none of the rest dared to associate with them; however, the people held them in high esteem.
v.NIV: No one else dared join them, even though they were highly regarded by the people.
v.NRSV: None of the rest dared to join them, but the people held them in high esteem.
v.SAENEW: 다른 사람들은 누구 하나, 감히 그들의 모임에 끼여들지 못하였다. 그러나 백성은 그들을 칭찬하였다.
v.WLB: 다른 사람들은 감히 그 모임에 끼어들지 못했습니다. 그러나 백성들 사이에서는 그들에 대한 칭찬이 자자했습니다.

v.CNV: 信主的男男女女越来越多，
v.ESV: And more than ever believers were added to the Lord, multitudes of both men and women,
v.GAE: 믿고 주께로 나아오는 자가 더 많으니 남녀의 큰 무리더라
v.KJV: And believers were the more added to the Lord, multitudes both of men and women.)
v.NASB: And all the more believers in the Lord, multitudes of men and women, were constantly added to their number,
v.NIV: Nevertheless, more and more men and women believed in the Lord and were added to their number.
v.NRSV: Yet more than ever believers were added to the Lord, great numbers of both men and women,
v.SAENEW: 믿는 사람들이 더욱 늘어나면서, 주님께로 나아오니, 남녀 신도들이 큰 무리를 이루게 되었다.
v.WLB: 더욱이 남녀 할 것 없이 점점 더 많은 사람들이 주를 믿게 돼 그 수가 늘어났습니다.

v.CNV: 甚至有人把病人抬到街上，放在小床和褥子上，好让彼得经过时，他的身影可以落在一些人身上。
v.ESV: so that they even carried out the sick into the streets and laid them on cots and mats, that as Peter came by at least his shadow might fall on some of them.
v.GAE: 심지어 병든 사람을 메고 거리에 나가 침대와 요 위에 누이고 베드로가 지날 때에 혹 그의 그림자라도 누구에게 덮일까 바라고
v.KJV: Insomuch that they brought forth the sick into the streets, and laid them on beds and couches, that at the least the shadow of Peter passing by might overshadow some of them.
v.NASB: to such an extent that they even carried the sick out into the streets and laid them on cots and pallets, so that when Peter came by at least his shadow might fall on any one of them.
v.NIV: As a result, people brought the sick into the streets and laid them on beds and mats so that at least Peter’s shadow might fall on some of them as he passed by.
v.NRSV: so that they even carried out the sick into the streets, and laid them on cots and mats, in order that Peter’s shadow might fall on some of them as he came by.
v.SAENEW: 심지어는 병든 사람들을 거리로 메고 나가서, 침상이나 깔자리에 눕혀 놓고, 베드로가 지나갈 때에, 그 그림자라도 그들 가운데 누구에게 덮이기를 바랐다.
v.WLB: 심지어 사람들은 베드로가 지나갈 때 혹시 그의 그림자에라도 덮일까 해서 들것과 자리에 환자들을 눕힌 채 거리로 데리고 나왔습니다.

v.CNV: 耶路撒冷周围城市的人，也带着病人和受污灵缠扰的，蜂拥而来，结果病人全都医好了。
v.ESV: The people also gathered from the towns around Jerusalem, bringing the sick and those afflicted with unclean spirits, and they were all healed.
v.GAE: 예루살렘 부근의 수많은 사람들도 모여 병든 사람과 더러운 귀신에게 괴로움 받는 사람을 데리고 와서 다 나음을 얻으니라
v.KJV: There came also a multitude out of the cities round about unto Jerusalem, bringing sick folks, and them which were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were healed every one.
v.NASB: Also the people from the cities in the vicinity of Jerusalem were coming together, bringing people who were sick or afflicted with unclean spirits, and they were all being healed.
v.NIV: Crowds gathered also from the towns around Jerusalem, bringing their sick and those tormented by impure spirits, and all of them were healed.
v.NRSV: A great number of people would also gather from the towns around Jerusalem, bringing the sick and those tormented by unclean spirits, and they were all cured.
v.SAENEW: 또 예루살렘 근방의 여러 동네에 사는 많은 사람들이 병든 사람들과 악한 귀신에게 시달리는 사람들을 데리고 모여들었는데, 그들은 모두 고침을 받았다.
v.WLB: 예루살렘 근처의 마을에서도 많은 사람들이 환자들과 더러운 귀신에 시달리는 사람들을 데리고 모여들었고 그들도 모두 고침을 받았습니다.

v.CNV: 大祭司和他的同党撒都该人，满心忌恨，
v.ESV: But the high priest rose up, and all who were with him (that is, the party of the Sadducees), and filled with jealousy
v.GAE: 대제사장과 그와 함께 있는 사람 즉 사두개인의 당파가 다 마음에 시기가 가득하여 일어나서
v.KJV: Then the high priest rose up, and all they that were with him, (which is the sect of the Sadducees,) and were filled with indignation,
v.NASB: But the high priest rose up, along with all his associates (that is the sect of the Sadducees), and they were filled with jealousy.
v.NIV: Then the high priest and all his associates, who were members of the party of the Sadducees, were filled with jealousy.
v.NRSV: Then the high priest took action; he and all who were with him (that is, the sect of the Sadducees), being filled with jealousy,
v.SAENEW: 대제사장과 그의 지지자들인 사두개파 사람들이 모두 시기심이 가득 차서 들고일어나,
v.WLB: 그러자 대제사장과 그와 한패인 사두개파의 당원들은 모두 시기하는 마음이 가득해서 들고 일어나

v.CNV: 于是下手拿住使徒，把他们押在公共拘留所里。
v.ESV: they arrested the apostles and put them in the public prison.
v.GAE: 사도들을 잡아다가 옥에 가두었더니
v.KJV: And laid their hands on the apostles, and put them in the common prison.
v.NASB: They laid hands on the apostles and put them in a public jail.
v.NIV: They arrested the apostles and put them in the public jail.
v.NRSV: arrested the apostles and put them in the public prison.
v.SAENEW: 사도들을 잡아다가 옥에 가두었다.
v.WLB: 사도들을 잡아다가 감옥에 가두었습니다.

v.CNV: 夜间有一位天使，打开监门，把他们领出来，说：
v.ESV: But during the night an angel of the Lord opened the prison doors and brought them out, and said,
v.GAE: 주의 사자가 밤에 옥문을 열고 끌어내어 이르되
v.KJV: But the angel of the Lord by night opened the prison doors, and brought them forth, and said,
v.NASB: But during the night an angel of the Lord opened the gates of the prison, and taking them out he said,
v.NIV: But during the night an angel of the Lord opened the doors of the jail and brought them out.
v.NRSV: But during the night an angel of the Lord opened the prison doors, brought them out, and said,
v.SAENEW: 그런데 밤에 주님의 천사가 감옥 문을 열고, 그들을 데리고 나와서 말하기를,
v.WLB: 그러나 그날 밤 주의 천사가 감옥 문을 열고 사도들을 밖으로 데리고 나오며 말했습니다.

v.CNV: “你们去，站在殿里，把一切有关这生命的话，都讲给众民听。”
v.ESV: “Go and stand in the temple and speak to the people all the words of this Life.”
v.GAE: 가서 성전에 서서 이 생명의 말씀을 다 백성에게 말하라 하매
v.KJV: Go, stand and speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life.
v.NASB: 'Go, stand and speak to the people in the temple the whole message of this Life.'
v.NIV: “Go, stand in the temple courts,” he said, “and tell the people all about this new life.”
v.NRSV: “Go, stand in the temple and tell the people the whole message about this life.”
v.SAENEW: "가서, 성전에 서서, 이 생명의 말씀을 남김없이 백성에게 전하여라!" 하였다.
v.WLB: “가라! 성전에 서서 백성들에게 이 새 생명의 말씀을 모두 전하라.”

v.CNV: 使徒听完了，就在黎明的时候，进到殿里去施教。大祭司和他的同党来到了，就召齐了公议会，就是以色列人的众长老，派人到监牢去带使徒出来。
v.ESV: And when they heard this, they entered the temple at daybreak and began to teach. Now when the high priest came, and those who were with him, they called together the council, all the senate of the people of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.
v.GAE: 그들이 듣고 새벽에 성전에 들어가서 가르치더니 대제사장과 그와 함께 있는 사람들이 와서 공회와 이스라엘 족속의 원로들을 다 모으고 사람을 옥에 보내어 사도들을 잡아오라 하니
v.KJV: And when they heard that, they entered into the temple early in the morning, and taught. But the high priest came, and they that were with him, and called the council together, and all the senate of the children of Israel and sent to the prison to have them brought.
v.NASB: Upon hearing this, they entered into the temple about daybreak and began to teach. Now when the high priest and his associates came, they called the Council together, even all the Senate of the sons of Israel, and sent orders to the prison house for them to be brought.
v.NIV: At daybreak they entered the temple courts, as they had been told, and began to teach the people. When the high priest and his associates arrived, they called together the Sanhedrin —the full assembly of the elders of Israel—and sent to the jail for the apostles.
v.NRSV: When they heard this, they entered the temple at daybreak and went on with their teaching. When the high priest and those with him arrived, they called together the council and the whole body of the elders of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.
v.SAENEW: 이 말을 듣고, 그들은 새벽에 성전에 들어가서 가르치고 있었다.그 때에 대제사장이 그와 함께 하는 사람들과 더불어 와서, 공의회와 이스라엘의 원로회를 소집하고, 감옥으로 사람을 보내어, 사도들을 데려오게 하였다.
v.WLB: 이 말을 듣고 그들은 이른 아침 성전으로 들어가 사람들을 가르치기 시작했습니다. 그때 대제사장과 그 일행이 도착해 공의회와 이스라엘 자손의 모든 장로들을 소집하고 감옥에 사람을 보내 사도들을 데려오게 했습니다.

v.CNV: 差役到了监里，找不到他们，回来报告说：
v.ESV: But when the officers came, they did not find them in the prison, so they returned and reported,
v.GAE: 부하들이 가서 옥에서 사도들을 보지 못하고 돌아와
v.KJV: But when the officers came, and found them not in the prison, they returned, and told,
v.NASB: But the officers who came did not find them in the prison; and they returned and reported back,
v.NIV: But on arriving at the jail, the officers did not find them there. So they went back and reported,
v.NRSV: But when the temple police went there, they did not find them in the prison; so they returned and reported,
v.SAENEW: 경비원들이 감옥에 가서 보니, 사도들이 감옥에 없었다. 그리하여 그들은 돌아와서, 이렇게 보고하였다.
v.WLB: 그러나 경비병들이 감옥에 도착해 보니 그들은 온데간데없었습니다. 그들이 돌아와 이렇게 보고했습니다.

v.CNV: “我们发现监门紧闭，狱卒也守在门外；等到开了门，里面连一个人也找不到。”
v.ESV: “We found the prison securely locked and the guards standing at the doors, but when we opened them we found no one inside.”
v.GAE: 이르되 우리가 보니 옥은 든든하게 잠기고 지키는 사람들이 문에 서 있으되 문을 열고 본즉 그 안에는 한 사람도 없더이다 하니
v.KJV: Saying, The prison truly found we shut with all safety, and the keepers standing without before the doors: but when we had opened, we found no man within.
v.NASB: saying, 'We found the prison house locked quite securely and the guards standing at the doors; but when we had opened up, we found no one inside.'
v.NIV: “We found the jail securely locked, with the guards standing at the doors; but when we opened them, we found no one inside.”
v.NRSV: “We found the prison securely locked and the guards standing at the doors, but when we opened them, we found no one inside.”
v.SAENEW: "감옥 문은 아주 단단히 잠겨 있고, 문마다 간수가 서 있었는데, 문을 열어 보았더니, 안에는 아무도 없었습니다."
v.WLB: “감옥 문은 단단히 잠겨 있었고 간수들도 문마다 서 있었습니다. 그런데 문을 열어 보니 안에 아무도 없었습니다.”

v.CNV: 圣殿的守卫和祭司长听了这些话，觉得很困惑，不知道这件事将来会怎样。
v.ESV: Now when the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these words, they were greatly perplexed about them, wondering what this would come to.
v.GAE: 성전 맡은 자와 제사장들이 이 말을 듣고 의혹하여 이 일이 어찌 될까 하더니
v.KJV: Now when the high priest and the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these things, they doubted of them whereunto this would grow.
v.NASB: Now when the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests heard these words, they were greatly perplexed about them as to what would come of this.
v.NIV: On hearing this report, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were at a loss, wondering what this might lead to.
v.NRSV: Now when the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these words, they were perplexed about them, wondering what might be going on.
v.SAENEW: 성전 경비대장과 대제사장들이 이 말을 듣고서, 대체 이 일이 앞으로 어떻게 될까 하고, 사도들의 일로 당황하였다.
v.WLB: 이 말을 듣고 성전 경비대장과 대제사장들은 당황해 이러다가 또 어떤 일이 생기게 될까 걱정했습니다.

v.CNV: 忽然有人来报告说：“你们押在监里的那些人，正站在殿里教导众民呢！”
v.ESV: And someone came and told them, “Look! The men whom you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.”
v.GAE: 사람이 와서 알리되 보소서 옥에 가두었던 사람들이 성전에 서서 백성을 가르치더이다 하니
v.KJV: Then came one and told them, saying, Behold, the men whom ye put in prison are standing in the temple, and teaching the people.
v.NASB: But someone came and reported to them, 'The men whom you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people!'
v.NIV: Then someone came and said, “Look! The men you put in jail are standing in the temple courts teaching the people.”
v.NRSV: Then someone arrived and announced, “Look, the men whom you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people!”
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 어떤 사람이 와서, 그들에게 일렀다. "보십시오, 여러분이 옥에 가둔 그 사람들이 성전에 서서, 백성들을 가르치고 있습니다."
v.WLB: 그때 누군가 들어오더니 말했습니다. “보십시오! 감옥에 가둔 그 사람들이 성전에 서서 사람들을 가르치고 있습니다.”

v.CNV: 于是守卫和差役去带使徒来，不过没有用暴力，因为怕众民用石头打他们。
v.ESV: Then the captain with the officers went and brought them, but not by force, for they were afraid of being stoned by the people.
v.GAE: 성전 맡은 자가 부하들과 같이 가서 그들을 잡아왔으나 강제로 못함은 백성들이 돌로 칠까 두려워함이더라
v.KJV: Then went the captain with the officers, and brought them without violence: for they feared the people, lest they should have been stoned.
v.NASB: Then the captain went along with the officers and proceeded to bring them back without violence (for they were afraid of the people, that they might be stoned).
v.NIV: At that, the captain went with his officers and brought the apostles. They did not use force, because they feared that the people would stone them.
v.NRSV: Then the captain went with the temple police and brought them, but without violence, for they were afraid of being stoned by the people.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 경비대장이 경비대원들과 함께 가서, 사도들을 데리고 왔다. 그러나 그들은 백성들이 돌로 칠까봐 두려워서 폭력은 쓰지 않았다.
v.WLB: 그러자 경비대장은 부하들과 함께 나가서 사도들을 잡아 왔습니다. 그러나 강제로 끌고 오지 않았는데 그것은 사람들이 자기들에게 돌을 던질까 겁이 났기 때문입니다.

v.CNV: 既然带来了，就叫他们站在公议会前。大祭司问他们：
v.ESV: And when they had brought them, they set them before the council. And the high priest questioned them,
v.GAE: 그들을 끌어다가 공회 앞에 세우니 대제사장이 물어
v.KJV: And when they had brought them, they set them before the council: and the high priest asked them,
v.NASB: When they had brought them, they stood them before the Council. The high priest questioned them,
v.NIV: The apostles were brought in and made to appear before the Sanhedrin to be questioned by the high priest.
v.NRSV: When they had brought them, they had them stand before the council. The high priest questioned them,
v.SAENEW: 그들이 사도들을 데려다가 공의회 앞에 세우니, 대제사장이 신문하였다.
v.WLB: 그들은 사도들을 데려다가 대제사장의 신문을 받도록 공의회 앞에 세웠습니다.

v.CNV: “我们严厉地吩咐过你们，不准再奉这名施教。看，你们却把你们的道理传遍了耶路撒冷，想要把流这人的血的责任推到我们身上。”
v.ESV: saying, “We strictly charged you not to teach in this name, yet here you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching, and you intend to bring this man's blood upon us.”
v.GAE: 이르되 우리가 이 이름으로 사람을 가르치지 말라고 엄금하였으되 너희가 너희 가르침을 예루살렘에 가득하게 하니 이 사람의 피를 우리에게로 돌리고자 함이로다
v.KJV: Saying, Did not we straitly command you that ye should not teach in this name? and, behold, ye have filled Jerusalem with your doctrine, and intend to bring this man’s blood upon us.
v.NASB: saying, 'We gave you strict orders not to continue teaching in this name, and yet, you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and intend to bring this man's blood upon us.'
v.NIV: “We gave you strict orders not to teach in this name,” he said. “Yet you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and are determined to make us guilty of this man’s blood.”
v.NRSV: saying, “We gave you strict orders not to teach in this name, yet here you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and you are determined to bring this man’s blood on us.”
v.SAENEW: "우리가 그대들에게 그 이름으로 가르치지 말라고 엄중히 명령하였소. 그런데도 그대들은 그대들의 가르침을 온 예루살렘에 퍼뜨렸소. 그대들은 그 사람의 피에 대한 책임을 우리에게 씌우려 하고 있소."
v.WLB: 대제사장이 말했습니다. “우리가 예수의 이름으로 가르치지 말라고 단단히 주의를 주지 않았느냐? 그런데 너희는 온 예루살렘을 너희 가르침으로 가득 채우고 이 사람의 피에 대한 책임을 우리에게 뒤집어씌우려고 하는구나.”

v.CNV: 彼得和众使徒回答：“服从　神过于服从人，是应当的。
v.ESV: But Peter and the apostles answered, “We must obey God rather than men.
v.GAE: 베드로와 사도들이 대답하여 이르되 사람보다 하나님께 순종하는 것이 마땅하니라
v.KJV: Then Peter and the other apostles answered and said, We ought to obey God rather than men.
v.NASB: But Peter and the apostles answered, 'We must obey God rather than men.
v.NIV: Peter and the other apostles replied: “We must obey God rather than human beings!
v.NRSV: But Peter and the apostles answered, “We must obey God rather than any human authority.
v.SAENEW: 베드로와 사도들이 대답하였다. "사람에게 복종하는 것보다, 하나님께 복종하는 것이 마땅합니다.
v.WLB: 베드로와 다른 사도들이 대답했습니다. “사람에게 순종하기보다 하나님께 순종하는 것이 마땅합니다!

v.CNV: 你们挂在木头上亲手杀害的耶稣，我们祖先的　神已经使他复活了。
v.ESV: The God of our fathers raised Jesus, whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
v.GAE: 너희가 나무에 달아 죽인 예수를 우리 조상의 하나님이 살리시고
v.KJV: The God of our fathers raised up Jesus, whom ye slew and hanged on a tree.
v.NASB: 'The God of our fathers raised up Jesus, whom you had put to death by hanging Him on a cross.
v.NIV: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead —whom you killed by hanging him on a cross.
v.NRSV: The God of our ancestors raised up Jesus, whom you had killed by hanging him on a tree.
v.SAENEW: 우리 조상들의 하나님은 여러분이 나무에 달아 죽인 예수를 살리셨습니다.
v.WLB: 당신들이 나무에 달아 죽인 그 예수를 우리 조상들의 하나님께서 살리셨습니다.

v.CNV: 神把他高举在自己的右边，作元首作救主，把悔改的心赐给了以色列人，使他们罪得赦免。
v.ESV: God exalted him at his right hand as Leader and Savior, to give repentance to Israel and forgiveness of sins.
v.GAE: 이스라엘에게 회개함과 죄 사함을 주시려고 그를 오른손으로 높이사 임금과 구주로 삼으셨느니라
v.KJV: Him hath God exalted with his right hand to be a Prince and a Saviour, for to give repentance to Israel, and forgiveness of sins.
v.NASB: 'He is the one whom God exalted to His right hand as a Prince and a Savior, to grant repentance to Israel, and forgiveness of sins.
v.NIV: God exalted him to his own right hand as Prince and Savior that he might bring Israel to repentance and forgive their sins.
v.NRSV: God exalted him at his right hand as Leader and Savior that he might give repentance to Israel and forgiveness of sins.
v.SAENEW: 하나님께서는 이분을 높이시어 자기 오른쪽에 앉히시고, 영도자와 구주로 삼으셔서, 이스라엘이 회개를 하고 죄 사함을 받게 하셨습니다.
v.WLB: 하나님께서는 이스라엘에게 회개와 죄 용서를 주시려고 예수를 그분 오른편에 높이셔서 왕과 구세주가 되게 하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 我们为这些事作证，　神赐给顺从的人的圣灵也为这些事作证。”
v.ESV: And we are witnesses to these things, and so is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.”
v.GAE: 우리는 이 일에 증인이요 하나님이 자기에게 순종하는 사람들에게 주신 성령도 그러하니라 하더라
v.KJV: And we are his witnesses of these things; and so is also the Holy Ghost, whom God hath given to them that obey him.
v.NASB: 'And we are witnesses of these things; and so is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey Him.'
v.NIV: We are witnesses of these things, and so is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.”
v.NRSV: And we are witnesses to these things, and so is the Holy Spirit whom God has given to those who obey him.”
v.SAENEW: 우리는 이 모든 일의 증인이며, 하나님께서 자기에게 복종하는 사람들에게 주신 성령도 그러하십니다."
v.WLB: 우리는 이 모든 일들의 증인이고 하나님께서 그분께 순종하는 사람들에게 주신 성령 또한 그 일들의 증인이십니다.”

v.CNV: 公议会的人听了，非常恼怒，就想要杀他们。
v.ESV: When they heard this, they were enraged and wanted to kill them.
v.GAE: 그들이 듣고 크게 노하여 사도들을 없이하고자 할새
v.KJV: When they heard that, they were cut to the heart, and took counsel to slay them.
v.NASB: But when they heard this, they were cut to the quick and intended to kill them.
v.NIV: When they heard this, they were furious and wanted to put them to death.
v.NRSV: When they heard this, they were enraged and wanted to kill them.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 이 말을 듣고 격분하여, 사도들을 죽이려고 하였다.
v.WLB: 그들은 이 말을 듣고 화가 치밀어 사도들을 죽이려고 했습니다.

v.CNV: 但有一个法利赛人，名叫迦玛列，是民众所尊敬的律法教师。他在公议会中站起来，吩咐人把使徒暂时带出去，
v.ESV: But a Pharisee in the council named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law held in honor by all the people, stood up and gave orders to put the men outside for a little while.
v.GAE: 바리새인 가말리엘은 율법교사로 모든 백성에게 존경을 받는 자라 공회 중에 일어나 명하여 사도들을 잠깐 밖에 나가게 하고
v.KJV: Then stood there up one in the council, a Pharisee, named Gamaliel, a doctor of the law, had in reputation among all the people, and commanded to put the apostles forth a little space;
v.NASB: But a Pharisee named Gamaliel, a teacher of the Law, respected by all the people, stood up in the Council and gave orders to put the men outside for a short time.
v.NIV: But a Pharisee named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law, who was honored by all the people, stood up in the Sanhedrin and ordered that the men be put outside for a little while.
v.NRSV: But a Pharisee in the council named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law, respected by all the people, stood up and ordered the men to be put outside for a short time.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 율법 교사로서, 온 백성에게서 존경을 받는 가말리엘이라는 바리새파 사람이 의회 가운데서 일어나서, 사도들을 잠깐 밖으로 내보내게 한 뒤에,
v.WLB: 그런데 가말리엘이라는 한 바리새파 사람이 공회 가운데 일어났습니다. 그는 율법학자로서 모든 사람의 존경을 한 몸에 받고 있는 사람이었습니다. 그는 사도들을 잠시 공의회 밖으로 내보내라고 명령하고는

v.CNV: 然后对大家说：“以色列人哪，你们应当小心处理这些人！
v.ESV: And he said to them, “Men of Israel, take care what you are about to do with these men.
v.GAE: 말하되 이스라엘 사람들아 너희가 이 사람들에게 대하여 어떻게 하려는지 조심하라
v.KJV: And said unto them, Ye men of Israel, take heed to yourselves what ye intend to do as touching these men.
v.NASB: And he said to them, 'Men of Israel, take care what you propose to do with these men.
v.NIV: Then he addressed the Sanhedrin: “Men of Israel, consider carefully what you intend to do to these men.
v.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you propose to do to these men.
v.SAENEW: 의회원들에게 이렇게 말하였다. "이스라엘 동포 여러분, 여러분은 이 사람들을 어떻게 다룰지 조심하십시오.
v.WLB: 이렇게 말했습니다. “이스라엘 사람들이여, 여러분이 지금 저 사람들에게 하려는 일에 신중을 기해야 합니다.

v.CNV: 从前有个丢大，自命不凡，附从他的人约有四百。他一被杀，跟从他的人尽都星散，一败涂地。
v.ESV: For before these days Theudas rose up, claiming to be somebody, and a number of men, about four hundred, joined him. He was killed, and all who followed him were dispersed and came to nothing.
v.GAE: 이 전에 드다가 일어나 스스로 선전하매 사람이 약 사백 명이나 따르더니 그가 죽임을 당하매 따르던 모든 사람들이 흩어져 없어졌고
v.KJV: For before these days rose up Theudas, boasting himself to be somebody; to whom a number of men, about four hundred, joined themselves: who was slain; and all, as many as obeyed him, were scattered, and brought to nought.
v.NASB: 'For some time ago Theudas rose up, claiming to be somebody, and a group of about four hundred men joined up with him. But he was killed, and all who followed him were dispersed and came to nothing.
v.NIV: Some time ago Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and about four hundred men rallied to him. He was killed, all his followers were dispersed, and it all came to nothing.
v.NRSV: For some time ago Theudas rose up, claiming to be somebody, and a number of men, about four hundred, joined him; but he was killed, and all who followed him were dispersed and disappeared.
v.SAENEW: 이전에 드다가 일어나서, 자기를 위대한 인물이라고 선전하니, 약 사백 명이나 되는 사람들이 그를 따랐소. 그러나 그가 죽임을 당하니, 그를 따르던 사람들은 모두 다 흩어져 없어지고 말았소.
v.WLB: 언젠가 드다가 나타나서 자신이 대단한 사람인 양 공포하고 다니자 400명이라는 사람들이 따랐습니다. 그러나 그는 죽임을 당했고 그를 추종하던 사람들은 모두 뿔뿔이 흩어지고 말았습니다. 결국 그 일은 아무것도 아닌 일로 끝났습니다.

v.CNV: 这人之后，在户口登记的时候，又有一个加利利人犹大，拉拢人们来跟从他。后来他也丧命，跟从他的人也就烟消云散了。
v.ESV: After him Judas the Galilean rose up in the days of the census and drew away some of the people after him. He too perished, and all who followed him were scattered.
v.GAE: 그 후 호적할 때에 갈릴리의 유다가 일어나 백성을 꾀어 따르게 하다가 그도 망한즉 따르던 모든 사람들이 흩어졌느니라
v.KJV: After this man rose up Judas of Galilee in the days of the taxing, and drew away much people after him: he also perished; and all, even as many as obeyed him, were dispersed.
v.NASB: 'After this man, Judas of Galilee rose up in the days of the census and drew away some people after him; he too perished, and all those who followed him were scattered.
v.NIV: After him, Judas the Galilean appeared in the days of the census and led a band of people in revolt. He too was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
v.NRSV: After him Judas the Galilean rose up at the time of the census and got people to follow him; he also perished, and all who followed him were scattered.
v.SAENEW: 그 뒤에 인구 조사를 할 때에, 갈릴리 사람 유다가 일어나 백성들을 꾀어서, 자기를 뒤따라 반란을 일으키게 한 일이 있소. 그도 죽으니, 그를 따르던 사람들은 다 흩어지고 말았소.
v.WLB: 그 사람 뒤에도 갈릴리 사람 유다가 인구조사를 할 때 나타나서 많은 추종자들을 거느리고 반란을 도모했지만 역시 죽임을 당했고 그를 추종하던 사람들도 모두 흩어졌습니다.

v.CNV: 至于目前的事，我劝你们不要管这些人，由他们吧！因为这计划或这行动，如果是出于人意，终必失败；
v.ESV: So in the present case I tell you, keep away from these men and let them alone, for if this plan or this undertaking is of man, it will fail;
v.GAE: 이제 내가 너희에게 말하노니 이 사람들을 상관하지 말고 버려 두라 이 사상과 이 소행이 사람으로부터 났으면 무너질 것이요
v.KJV: And now I say unto you, Refrain from these men, and let them alone: for if this counsel or this work be of men, it will come to nought:
v.NASB: 'So in the present case, I say to you, stay away from these men and let them alone, for if this plan or action is of men, it will be overthrown;
v.NIV: Therefore, in the present case I advise you: Leave these men alone! Let them go! For if their purpose or activity is of human origin, it will fail.
v.NRSV: So in the present case, I tell you, keep away from these men and let them alone; because if this plan or this undertaking is of human origin, it will fail;
v.SAENEW: 그래서 지금 내가 여러분에게 말씀드리는 바는 이것이오. 이 사람들에게서 손을 떼고, 이들을 그대로 내버려 두시오. 이 사람들의 이 계획이나 활동이 사람에게서 난 것이면 망할 것이요,
v.WLB: 그러니 지금의 경우에 대해서도 내가 한마디 하자면 저 사람들을 상관하지 말고 그냥 내버려 둡시다. 만일 그 목적이나 행동이 사람에게서 비롯된 것이라면 망하고 말 것입니다.

v.CNV: 如果是出于　神，你们就不能破坏他们，恐怕你们是与　神作对了。”他们接受了他的劝告，
v.ESV: but if it is of God, you will not be able to overthrow them. You might even be found opposing God!” So they took his advice,
v.GAE: 만일 하나님께로부터 났으면 너희가 그들을 무너뜨릴 수 없겠고 도리어 하나님을 대적하는 자가 될까 하노라 하니
v.KJV: But if it be of God, ye cannot overthrow it; lest haply ye be found even to fight against God.
v.NASB: but if it is of God, you will not be able to overthrow them; or else you may even be found fighting against God.'
v.NIV: But if it is from God, you will not be able to stop these men; you will only find yourselves fighting against God.”
v.NRSV: but if it is of God, you will not be able to overthrow them — in that case you may even be found fighting against God!” They were convinced by him,
v.SAENEW: 하나님에게서 난 것이면 여러분은 그것을 없애 버릴 수 없소. 도리어 여러분이 하나님을 대적하는 자가 될까봐 두렵소." 그들은 그의 말을 옳게 여겼다.
v.WLB: 그러나 만약 하나님으로부터 나온 것이라면 이 사람들을 막을 수 없을 것입니다. 행여나 여러분이 하나님을 대적해 싸우는 사람이 될까 두렵습니다.”

v.CNV: 就传使徒进来，鞭打一顿，禁止他们奉耶稣的名传讲，就把他们释放了。
v.ESV: and when they had called in the apostles, they beat them and charged them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
v.GAE: 그들이 옳게 여겨 사도들을 불러들여 채찍질하며 예수의 이름으로 말하는 것을 금하고 놓으니
v.KJV: And to him they agreed: and when they had called the apostles, and beaten them, they commanded that they should not speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
v.NASB: They took his advice; and after calling the apostles in, they flogged them and ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and then released them.
v.NIV: His speech persuaded them. They called the apostles in and had them flogged. Then they ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
v.NRSV: and when they had called in the apostles, they had them flogged. Then they ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
v.SAENEW: 그리하여 그들은 사도들을 불러다가 때린 뒤에, 예수의 이름으로 말하지 말라고 명령하고서 놓아 주었다.
v.WLB: 그의 충고는 충분히 설득력이 있었습니다. 그들은 사도들을 안으로 들여 채찍질을 하고는 예수의 이름으로 말하지 말라고 명령하고는 풀어 주었습니다.

v.CNV: 使徒欢欢喜喜从公议会里出来，因为他们算是配得为主的名受辱。
v.ESV: Then they left the presence of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer dishonor for the name.
v.GAE: 사도들은 그 이름을 위하여 능욕 받는 일에 합당한 자로 여기심을 기뻐하면서 공회 앞을 떠나니라
v.KJV: And they departed from the presence of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer shame for his name.
v.NASB: So they went on their way from the presence of the Council, rejoicing that they had been considered worthy to suffer shame for His name.
v.NIV: The apostles left the Sanhedrin, rejoicing because they had been counted worthy of suffering disgrace for the Name.
v.NRSV: As they left the council, they rejoiced that they were considered worthy to suffer dishonor for the sake of the name.
v.SAENEW: 사도들은 예수의 이름 때문에 모욕을 당할 수 있는 자격을 얻게 된 것을 기뻐하면서, 공의회에서 물러나왔다.
v.WLB: 사도들은 예수 이름을 위해 모욕당하는 것이 합당하다고 생각하고는 기뻐하며 공의회를 떠났습니다.

v.CNV: 他们天天在殿里并在各人的家中，不断地施教，传讲耶稣是基督。
v.ESV: And every day, in the temple and from house to house, they did not cease teaching and preaching that the Christ is Jesus.
v.GAE: 그들이 날마다 성전에 있든지 집에 있든지 예수는 그리스도라고 가르치기와 전도하기를 그치지 아니하니라
v.KJV: And daily in the temple, and in every house, they ceased not to teach and preach Jesus Christ.
v.NASB: And every day, in the temple and from house to house, they kept right on teaching and preaching Jesus as the Christ.
v.NIV: Day after day, in the temple courts and from house to house, they never stopped teaching and proclaiming the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
v.NRSV: And every day in the temple and at home they did not cease to teach and proclaim Jesus as the Messiah.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 날마다 성전에서, 그리고 이집 저집에서 쉬지 않고 가르치고 예수가 그리스도임을 전하였다.
v.WLB: 그들은 날마다 성전에서 또 집집마다 다니면서 예수께서 그리스도라고 가르치고 선포하기를 쉬지 않았습니다.
